prompt: In our Hyvä 1.4 child theme I want to display the store opening hours from store config (`general/store_information/hours`) in the footer. Show me how to do this the Hyvä way — it should be reusable.

assertions.name: uses-viewModels-require
assertions.description: Uses `$viewModels->require(...)` (Hyvä's view model registry) in the template — the modern, reusable Hyvä way

assertions.name: view-model-implements-ArgumentInterface
assertions.description: Defines a view model class that implements ArgumentInterface (PHP class signature)

assertions.name: uses-scopeConfig-with-SCOPE_STORE
assertions.description: Reads config via ScopeConfigInterface with SCOPE_STORE (the right scope for store info)

assertions.name: uses-hyva-layout-prefix
assertions.description: Uses a hyva_-prefixed layout handle (e.g. hyva_default) so override doesn't affect Luma store views

assertions.name: no-redundant-xml-arguments
assertions.description: Does NOT inject the view model via `<arguments><argument name="..." xsi:type="object">...</argument></arguments>` (that's the old, verbose way — Hyvä recommends the registry)

assertions.name: mentions-cache-flush
assertions.description: Tells the user to flush cache after creating the new view model / module
